Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The problem requires a DuckDB SQL query for an event log

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: WITH ordered_events AS SELECT case_id activity timestamp LEAD activity 1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_activity LEAD timestamp 1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next_timestamp LEAD activity 2 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next2_activity LEAD timestamp 2 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp AS next2_timestamp FROM event_log

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: bad_cases AS SELECT DISTINCT case_id FROM ordered_events WHERE activity = Approve Purchase Requisition AND next_activity = Create Order AND next2_activity = Confirm Order AND next2_timestamp next_timestamp more than 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id NOT IN SELECT case FROM bad_cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This query works by first creating a windowed view that looks ahead two events per case then identifies any case that contains the exact three activity sequence with the more than 5 day gap between the middle and last event and finally excludes every event belonging to those bad cases
